feed.title: SAP Community - Support Services
feed.subtitle: Support Services blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Beginners Guide: All about S-user ID
entry.content:
Got a New S-user ID from Your Company’s Super Admin? Here’s What You Need to Know!

When you receive a new S-user ID from your company’s super administrator and you’re eager to dive deep into how it all works, you’re in the right place. This SAP blog will guide you! 

 

What is an S-user ID? 

An S-user ID contains a 10-digit number beginning with the letter ‘S’ created by the Super Administrator of your company to allow you access to the SAP Support Portal and SAP for Me among other SAP support sites. Your S-user ID is evidence of your company’s support agreement with SAP and is connected to your company’s customer number.  

Depending on the authorizations assigned to you by your Super Administrator, you will be able to create cases, download software, chat with Technical Experts, Request License keys, view/access SAP Notes or Knowledge-Based Articles, and more in SAP for Me.

 

How to activate your new S-user ID?

When a super admin creates a new S-user ID, the new user will receive an e-mail from SAP Notification Services with the new S-user ID and an activation link, which is valid for 10 days. 

Steps to activate: 

  • Locate the email and click on the activation link provided. The link will redirect you to a secure page where you can set up your password. Or copy and paste the activation link into your browser.  

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Follow the instructions on the page to create a strong password.  
  • To verify your login access, you can go to Home - SAP for Me, enter your S-user ID “S00********” and password. 

Note: Make sure your password meets SAP's security requirements, which usually include a mix of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. If no activation email link is received, check your spam/junk folder. Ensure the email address associated with your S-user ID is correct.  

If you wish to link your new S-user ID to an SAP Universal ID, please refer to KBA: 3344809.

 

What if the Activation Email Link has Expired or Didn't Arrive? 

No need to worry! We got you. Users can manually request a new password reset link to activate their S-user ID using the below steps:   

Steps to Manually Reset your S-user ID password:  

  • Go to Forgot My Password 
  • Enter your S-user ID e.g. S00********** Tip: Entering an e-mail address is allowed but it is recommended to enter the S-user ID, especially if there are several S-user IDs linked to the e-mail address. 
  • Click Continue to Send 

 

  • If an account exists, an e-mail will be sent from SAP ID Service (ias@notifications.sap.com) to the e-mail address registered for the user.  
  • Open the e-mail to find the password reset link. 
  • Set up a new password. 
  • To verify your login access, you can go to Home - SAP for Me, enter your S-user ID “S00********” and password. 

Note:  

  • Password reset link is valid for 2 hours from the time it was generated from the system and can only be used once. If the password reset link expires before you reset the password, repeat the steps above. 
  • If no e-mail is received from ias@notifications.sap.com, make sure the S-user's e-mail address is valid and that it isn't being routed to the user's spam/junk e-mail folder.  
  • If users have issues performing the password reset (e.g. did not receive the e-mail or receive errors), send an e-mail to SAP ID Service at sso@sap.com. 
  • Account holders must change their own passwords; It's not possible for another user or admin to change another S-user's password. 
  • Passwords can only be changed once every 24 hours. 

More information about password changes and how to reset the S-user ID password is explained in this KBA: 1808560 - How to reset an S-user ID password.

 

For those S-user IDs that are linked by an SAP Universal ID (UID), the UID password needs to be reset by following the steps provided in this KBA: 2882938 - How to reset or change SAP Universal ID (UID) password. To learn more about SAP Universal IDs, you can refer here or, SAP Universal ID: Tips and Tricks - SAP Community.

 

Users will be able to access the SAP Support Portal and SAP for Me among other SAP support sites once the S-user ID is activated.  For more information regarding our new customer support portal “SAP for Me”, refer to this link: What is SAP for Me? - SAP Community or SAP for Me | SAP Community. 

 

How to request S-user ID authorization?  

S-user ID authorization is very important and is required to fulfill certain support-related tasks using the support applications that are offered in the SAP for Me. Most authorizations contain not only the user's right to perform a specific task but also the level at which the user can exercise this right.   

 

For more details, refer to the About the User, Authorization and Administrator Concept 

 

To request S-user ID authorizations, users need to contact their dedicated  Super, Cloud, or User Administrator for their customer number (see KBA: 1271517). Users can also use the Self-Service Authorization request feature in SAP for Me (see KBA: 2891528) to request authorizations. The request will be sent to administrators on the account (see Important Contacts) who can then accept or reject the request. 

Note: 

  • Administrators can only assign authorizations/levels that their own S-user ID has (see KBA:  2913833).  
  • An administrator cannot edit their own authorizations (see KBA:  2399897).

 

Can I transfer my S-user ID from one customer number to another customer number?

S-user IDs and Technical Communication S-User IDs cannot be moved (reassigned) to a different customer number. The data assigned to the S-user ID cannot be merged with another S-user ID. Each S-user ID and/or a Technical S-User ID belongs to the customer number where it was created. In addition, the customer (company) number associated with the ID cannot be changed. 

Refer to KBA: 2423500 for more details.  

 

Can I share my S-user ID with other users or groups?  

Each S-user ID created in SAP for Me User Management can only be assigned to one dedicated user.  S-user IDs are not to be shared by multiple users (e.g. Basis team, department, etc). In other words, the name and e-mail address for the S-user ID should match the user who is logging into SAP. 

It is very important that administrators double-check the user's information to ensure they are following this requirement:  

  • Each S-user ID is for an individual only, not groups. In other words, each S-user ID should have a First Name and Last Name (family name). 
  • Each S-user ID must have an e-mail address matching the individual's name and cannot have a group or shared e-mail address. 

If any users are currently using an S-user ID that doesn't match their individual name or e-mail address, administrators need to create a new S-user ID for the user(s) since name changes are not allowed in most cases (KBA 2597456). See the tasks administrators are responsible for in KBA 1282808. 

Note: If the requested new S-user has an e-mail address that matches an existing S-user ID (for a different company number), the first and last name will be used from that existing S-user. The name you entered on the Request User form will not be used. 

For more information regarding this process, refer to KBA: 3031039 - S-user ID cannot be shared by other users or groups.

In addition, if you need to learn more about S-user IDs, please click here. To learn more about different types of SAP User IDs, you may click here.  

 

Got More Questions? 

Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!   
 

Alternatively, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community.

 

 
Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts!

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming webcasts at https://events.sap.com/supportwebcasts/en/home. 
These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, Universal ID, etc. Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com.

 

Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance. 

 

Thanks for reading! We hope you found this useful.

entry.title: Enhancing SAP Support: Introducing Business Impact-Driven Case Prioritization
entry.content:
We would like to introduce a recent change we've made in the ‘Get Support’ application from SAP for Me. The key change is the shift from prioritizing issues based on levels to describing the business impact of the issue. This will directly determine the priority of your case, leading to more accurate and responsive support.

  • From Priority Levels to Impact Statements: Rather than selecting a priority level (low, medium, high, very high), you will now describe the business impact of your issue.
  • Impact-Driven Prioritization: The provided business impact will directly determine the priority of your case, ensuring a more accurate and responsive support experience.

A business impact statement is now mandatory for all 'moderately/seriously/critically affected' (medium, high and very high) cases.

To help you create an effective business impact statement, we've included helpful text prompts to guide you through the process.

By having a qualified business impact statement upfront, we can

  • Eliminate the back and forth typically needed to verify business impact, leading to faster case resolutions.
  • Ensure the right SAP engineers are assigned to your issues, optimizing support engineer resource allocation, and ensuring timely responses across different case priorities.

 

Related blogs:

  • Try out the newly designed ‘Case List’ to help you manage your support cases in SAP for Me
  • Improved ‘Get Support’ Experience: Enhanced Placement of Detailed Description & AI Recommendations
  • Try Out the Latest Enhancements in SAP for Me to Better Manage Your Support Cases
  • Why choosing the right system and product will help resolve your product issue faster

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention Summit: Uniting Thought Leaders for Success
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the SAP MaxAttention Summit is taking place in Munich, Germany, from April 13-15, 2025. This exclusive event is an onsite, in-person gathering designed for the SAP MaxAttention community to engage with thought leaders, SAP experts, and peers from some of the world's leading organizations.

What to expect (agenda at a glance)

From Sunday evening through Tuesday afternoon, the summit will feature captivating keynotes from SAP executives, customer experience sessions, and in-depth expert discussions. In addition, there will be a variety of networking opportunities, including a welcome reception, a special evening event, and one-on-one meetings.

SAP MaxAttention customer are invited to unleash their full business potential. Key features of the agenda for this exclusive SAP MaxAttention community include:

  • Future-focused strategy: Receive first-hand views into the product innovation roadmap, enabling a clearer way forward. You will have the opportunity to discuss with experts about the fully managed SaaS solution SAP Business Data Cloud. Learn more about the benefits and how it unifies and governs all SAP data seamlessly - unlocking new possibilities with SAP Business AI.

  • Best practice sharing: Hear from leading enterprises how they have successfully shifted from on-premise and hyperscaler environments to RISE with SAP and are now capitalizing on fast innovation cycles – guided by SAP MaxAttention. They will share their best practices using SAP Business AI and explain how the integrated toolchain with SAP Signavio, SAP LeanIX, and SAP Cloud ALM supports their transformation to SAP Business Suite.

  • Expert-guided architecture and automation: Leverage expertise in dialog-focused eXpert eXchange sessions to understand the details of designing your path to a robust enterprise architecture, enhancing business process automation and efficiency, harnessing AI-infused intelligence.

Moreover, the summit provides you with a chance to connect with SAP experts and members of the SAP Executive Board, get to know a select group of SAP's largest customers, build relationships, and gain valuable insights that can take your business to the next level.

Join Expert Speakers & Industry Leaders for Insightful Exchange

Please find below the already confirmed SAP speakers for the Summit. Among them are SAP Executive Board members and SAP executives. Further speakers from leading enterprises will soon be revealed on the event website.

Event Location: Munich Marriott Hotel City West

The Munich Marriott Hotel City West seamlessly blends sophistication with Bavarian charm in the vibrant Westend district. The hotel is located in the heart of Munich near the main train station. Featuring guest rooms and suites, along with meeting rooms with the latest audio-visual technology, and a state-of-the-art conference center, it provides an unparalleled experience for the Summit.

Welcome Reception (Sunday, April 13, Westhub Bar & Lounge, Munich Marriott Hotel City West)

As part of the hotel, the Westhub Bar & Lounge provides an elegant and modern setting for the official Welcome Reception of the Summit, to connect, to take advantage of the exceptional atmosphere, and to enhance your professional network - an ideal start for the Summit - all while indulging in an exquisite selection of drinks and delicious food.

Evening Event (Monday, April 14, SAP Garden)

The evening event takes place at the SAP Garden on Monday, April 14. This groundbreaking sports and event center, located in Munich's Olympic Park, opened in 2025 and is renowned for its modern design and technology-driven facilities supported by SAP innovations. The modern venue is home to ice hockey and basketball clubs, such as EHC Red Bull Munich and FC Bayern Munich. Participants will be offered an exquisite dinner while being entertained by captivating show acts.

Take a look at our booklet 

Check out our booklet including more information around the location, key topics and agenda now. 

Customers eligible to join this exclusive SAP MaxAttention event will be invited personally from January onwards.  

We can't wait to see you in Munich in April for what promises to be an unforgettable experience. Let's come together to learn, connect, and drive success! 

In case of questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out. 

Get inspired by the keynotes and sessions from the Summit 2024 from board members and executives as well as last year's blog post.

entry.title: #07 - Exploring Your SAP Solution: Engaging Stakeholders Early in the Implementation Process
entry.content:
Why is it important to familiarize yourself with your solution prior to implementation?

Let me share a story about a customer I worked with who took the time to enable their team before starting the project implementation and design discussions.

This customer was new to SAP and purchased a solution that was unfamiliar to them, yet critical for their business. As we recommend to all our customers, we advised them to undergo solution enablement before project implementation. This approach would help them become more familiar with the solution and maximize its value throughout the implementation process.

You’re probably wondering how it went for that customer. Well, I can tell you—it went really well!

When we began the design workshops, we presented the standard capabilities, as we always recommend to our customers for creating a scalable solution. However, we also recognize that business-specific requirements often call for adjustments. During these discussions, our customer was not only better prepared to engage with the topics and explanations we provided, but they were also able to identify key functionalities that would help enhance their business.

These design discussions are crucial during project implementation because they define the foundation of the customer’s solution. If the design is not done correctly from the start, the customer may end up with a solution that isn’t viable. Moreover, having the right solution design in place ensures that, during the testing phase, you're validating the solution effectively—rather than testing irrelevant processes that don’t align with the business needs. And that’s exactly what happened—my customer ended up with a solution that perfectly met their needs.

Another benefit was the time efficiency. By enabling themselves early on, the workshop discussions ran smoothly and stayed within the defined timelines, allowing us to progress without any delays. And it wasn’t just in the early stages where we were time-efficient; throughout all phases of the project implementation, we stayed on track because the scope of what needed to be delivered was clear from the start.

Finally, the entire team was engaged from day one and understood the impact of implementing a new solution. Typically, when a new solution is being implemented, not all users immediately embrace the change. Senior leadership is eager to move to a new system to improve business performance, increase margins and save costs, but business users are often resistant, especially when they are unfamiliar with the new system.

However, by enabling key stakeholders early on, we were able to make a significant impact on the skeptical business users. These stakeholders were able to champion the solution, sharing the added value it brought for the business users by streamlining their daily tasks. This empowered the business users to engage during the discussions and focus on higher-value activities, keeping them motivate. It also reinforced that everyone was contributing to the overall success of the business.

 

In a nutshell:

Gaining knowledge about SAP solution ahead of time is crucial, allows customers to maximize the value of the system from day one. 

By familiarizing themselves with the features, functionalities, and best practices before implementation, customers can ensure a smoother transition, reduce potential challenges, and align the solution more effectively with their business goals. This proactive approach allows teams to ask informed questions, make strategic decisions, and prepare their organization for a more seamless integration.

Furthermore, early exposure to the SAP solution empowers customers to take full advantage of its capabilities, ensuring they are ready to leverage its tools and insights to drive business success once the system goes live.

Stay curious and never stop learning!

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Ana Barreira serves as the Global Onboarding Advisor for the SAP Transportation Management (TM) solution within the Supply Chain Management area. She provides expert guidance and support to new SAP customers from contract signature through to successful go-live. With eleven years of experience—three of which have been as a Global Onboarding Advisor, and eight years as an SAP Consultant—Ana specializes in the SAP TM solution. She has successfully supported full-cycle customer implementation projects, assisted partners, and contributed to internal enablement efforts

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ana_Barreira  or @Linh-Hoang .

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: So wenden Sie sich an das SAP Education - Learning-Hub-Team um Unterstützung zu erhalten
entry.content:
** This blog was in collaboration/translation with my German speaking colleague Rebecca Dolan

Benötigen Sie Unterstützung vom Learning Hub/Schulungsteam erhalten und wissen nicht, wo Sie anfangen sollen? Dann ist dieser Blog für Sie…  

Hier zeige ich Ihnen die Schritte, um das SAP Learning Support Center zu kontaktieren. Die Support-Mitarbeiter sind Montag bis Freitag, rund um die Uhr verfügbar, und die meisten Anfragen werden innerhalb von 24 Stunden beantwortet. 

* Klicken Sie hier, wenn Sie dies auf Englisch, Italienisch oder Spanisch lesen möchten

 

In der Support-Plattform finden Sie auch häufig gestellte Fragen zu folgenden Themen:  

  • Allgemeine Support-Themen  
  • Praxissysteme  
  • Themen von Schulungs-Reseller-Partnern  
  • Lern- und Zertifizierungsberatung   
  • Kursbuchung und Bezahlung   
  • Und Analyse-Dashboard 

 Anlegen eines Falls  

Wenn Sie feststellen, dass Sie auf der Supportplattform nicht die gewünschten Informationen finden, können Sie einen Fall anlegen, um Unterstützung mit Ihrer Anfrage zu erhalten.   

Dazu müssen Sie sich lediglich mit Ihrer Universal ID anmelden und ein Ticket erstellen. 

Auf der SAP-Learning-Hub-Website:  

  1. Klicken Sie auf das Fragezeichen (?) rechts oben auf der Seite.
  2. Auf der Help-Center-Seite wählen Sie dann auf die Registerkarte „Contact Us“, und klicken dann auf „Request Technical Assistance“. 
  3. Klicken Sie dann auf „Need More Help“. 

     

  4. Melden Sie sich mit Ihrer Universal ID an, und klicken Sie dann auf "Create Case". 

 Auf der SAP-Learning-Website:  

  1. Scrollen Sie nach unten zum Ende der Seite, und klicken Sie unter „Support and Contact“ auf „Get Support“. 
  2. Nun werden Sie auf die User-Support-Center Webseite weitergeleitet. Dort finden Sie die FAQs zur Selbsthilfe und detaillierte Leitfäden.  
  3. Wenn der erforderliche Support nicht in den FAQs gefunden wird, scrollen Sie nach unten, und klicken auf das „Need Help“ Symbol. Sie werden dann zur nächsten Seite weitergeleitet, um ein Ticket zu erstellen.  
  4. Klicken Sie "Create Case". 

     

Darüber hinaus können Sie den direkten Link zum User-Support-Center verwenden.  

Um den Status Ihres Tickets zu sehen, klicken Sie auf den Refresh-Button, um den Status ihres Tickets zu aktualisieren. 

Worin können Supportmitarbeiter Sie unterstützen?  

Das Schulungsteam kann Sie unterstützen, wenn Sie Informationen zu Kursen, Zertifizierungsinhalten und Learning Journeys erhalten möchten. Außerdem kann es Sie beraten, um zu verstehen, welche Schulungen erforderlich sind, um Ihre Ziele zu erreichen.  

Weitere Leistungen sind:   

  • Fragen zur Zertifizierung, einschließlich Ratschläge zur korrekten UID-Einrichtung und wie Sie auf Ihre Badges zugreifen können.   
  • Probleme im Zusammenhang mit Informations-, Liefer- und Nachsorgeleistungen von Unterrichtsräumen mit Kursleiter.  
  • Buchungen und abrechnungsbezogene Probleme werden in der Regel von einem anderen internen Team bearbeitet, aber das Learning-Team kann sich einbringen, um die Genehmigung für kostenlose Umbuchungen und ähnliche Szenarien zu erteilen, da diese häufig mit Kundenreklamationen usw. zusammenhängen.  
  • Visabriefe.  
  • Bereitstellen von Lieferanteninformationen auf Anforderung. 

Fordern Sie Unterstützung in einer anderen Sprache an.  

Wenn Sie möchten, dass das Schulungsteam Sie in Ihrer bevorzugten Sprache unterstützt, können Sie die Sprache auswählen, nachdem Sie die Beschreibung hinzugefügt haben. Sie müssen das Land auswählen, in dem Sie sich befinden, und zwischen Deutsch, Englisch, Spanisch, Französisch und Japanisch wählen. 

Zusätzlich empfehlen wir Ihnen den Blog  SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! Um mehr zum Them SAP-Zertifizierungen und UID zu erfahren. 

Der folgende Wissensdatenbankartikel kann auch hilfreich sein: 2404504 - Hilfe zum SAP Learning Hub oder SAP Training Center 

Danke fürs Lesen!

entry.title: Cómo comunicarse con el equipo de SAP Education - Learning Hub para obtener ayuda
entry.content:
¿Está buscando obtener ayuda del SAP Education/Learning Hub y no sabe por dónde empezar? Entonces este blog es para usted... 

Aquí compartiré los pasos que debes seguir para interactuar con el área de soporte; los agentes están disponibles las 24 horas del día, de lunes a viernes, y la mayoría de las consultas se responden dentro de las 24 horas. 

* Haga click aqui si desea leer este blog en Alemán, Italiano o en Inglés

En  la plataforma de soporte también podrás encontrar preguntas frecuentes relacionadas con: 

  • Temas de soporte general 
  • Sistemas de práctica 
  • Temas de capacitación para socios revendedores 
  • Asesoramiento sobre aprendizaje y certificación  
  • Reserva y pago de cursos  
  • Y panel de análisis 

Cómo crear un caso 

Si la información que está buscando no está en la plataforma, puede crear un caso para obtener ayuda sobre su consulta específica.  

Todo lo que necesita hacer es iniciar sesión con su Universal ID y crear un ticket. 

Desde el sitio SAP Learning Hub : 

  1. Debe hacer clic en el ícono del signo de interrogación (?) que se encuentra en la parte superior derecha de la página. 
  2. En la página del Centro de ayuda, haga clic en el botón “Contáctenos” y seleccione “Solicitar asistencia técnica”
     
  3. Haga clic en "¿Necesita más ayuda?" 
  4. Inicie sesión con su ID Universal y luego deberá hacer clic en "Crear caso" 

Desde el sitio de aprendizaje de SAP : 

  1. Desplácese hasta la parte inferior de la página y haga clic en “Obtener soporte”, que se encuentra en Soporte y contacto. 
  2. Será dirigido a la siguiente página, la página del Centro de soporte al usuario, donde podrá encontrar las preguntas frecuentes de autoayuda y guías detalladas. 
  3. Si no encuentra la ayuda que necesita en las preguntas frecuentes, desplácese hacia abajo y haga clic en el ícono "¿Necesita ayuda?" y será dirigido a la página siguiente para generar un ticket. 
  4. Haga clic en el botón “Crear caso”. 

Además, puede utilizar el enlace directo al Centro de soporte al usuario . 

Para ver la actualización de su ticket: haga clic en el botón Actualizar antes de encontrar la actualización de su caso/ticket. 

¿En qué pueden ayudarle los agentes de soporte? 

El Equipo de Aprendizaje puede ayudarle si desea obtener información sobre cursos, contenido de certificación y rutas de aprendizaje. También pueden asesorarle para que comprenda qué aprendizaje es necesario para lograr sus objetivos. 

Otros servicios incluyen:  

  • Preguntas de primer nivel sobre certificación, incluido asesoramiento sobre la configuración correcta del UID y cómo acceder a sus credenciales.  
  • Cuestiones relacionadas con la información, la prestación y el servicio posterior a las clases dirigidas por instructores. 
  • Los problemas relacionados con las reservas y la facturación generalmente los maneja otro equipo interno, pero el equipo de aprendizaje puede involucrarse para dar aprobación para cualquier nueva reserva gratuita y situaciones similares, ya que a menudo están relacionadas con quejas de los clientes, etc. 
  • Cartas de visa. 
  • Proporcionar información de proveedores a pedido. 

Solicitar soporte en un idioma diferente. 

Si desea que el equipo de aprendizaje lo ayude en su idioma preferido, puede seleccionar el idioma una vez que haya agregado la descripción, deberá elegir el país en el que se encuentra y seleccionar entre: Alemán, Inglés, Español, Francés y Japonés. 

También nos gustaría compartir este blog para que puedas aprender más sobre las certificaciones SAP y UID... ¡todo lo que necesitas saber! Y este KBA también puede ser útil:  2404504 - Cómo obtener ayuda con SAP Learning Hub o SAP Training Center 

¡Gracias por leer!

entry.title: Replays Available: SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024
entry.content:
SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024 took place on November 13 and 14. 

This was an exclusive virtual event designed to provide valuable insights and strategies to elevate your business transformation through SAP’s comprehensive digital support experience. 

If you could not attend the event or would like to review specific sessions, you can access all materials such as replays, presentations, and consolidated FAQs here. 

All sessions are available in multiple languages: English, German, Spanish, Portuguese, Chinese and Japanese. 

 Here is a glimpse of what's available for you: 

  • Discover how SAP is revolutionizing customer support with AI to strengthen your business. 
  • Explore real-time AI applications with Real-Time Support. 
  • Uncover powerful strategies for optimizing cloud and hybrid solutions with SAP Cloud ALM and the SAP Business Transformation Center. 
  • See how SAP Ariba can simplify and streamline procurement processes. 
  • Boost your RISE with SAP project with our tailored SAP Enterprise Support offerings. 
  • Accelerate your SAP Business Technology Platform implementation. 
  • Drive transformation using SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX. 
  • Deepen your understanding of SAP HCM and explore ways to enhance employee engagement.

entry.title: The Importance of Sharing Enhancement Ideas with SAP for ME
entry.content:
SAP is committed to continuous improvement, and customer feedback is a vital and valued part of this process. By sharing your ideas, you contribute to the evolution of SAP for ME, making it more powerful and user-friendly.

How to share your Enhancement ideas? Well, that’s easy. All you need to do is to follow these steps:

  • Get started by opening the SAP Customer Influence Open Influence Opportunities - Customer Influence
  • Log on with the S-user and accept the terms of use.
  • Navigate to the SAP for Me Continuous Influence Session (It is recommended to bookmark this link on favorite bar).
  • Search if the idea has already been submitted by another customer and if not, then log the idea.
  • Vote for Ideas that are supported and planned for implementation within the organization. Notifications will be sent about updates on voted ideas.
  • Submit Idea: If an idea that matches the requirement cannot be found, the 'Submit Improvement' button should be used to create a new idea and the relevant template should be filled out.
  • Wait for Review: Once an idea is submitted, it becomes visible to other customers and can be voted on. Ideas that reach the voting threshold (minimum number of votes) will be reviewed.
  • Voting threshold: Each product session may have different thresholds. This information can be found on the session landing page of each product.
  • Ideas that have little to no votes from the community are automatically closed to help ease of navigation & visibility for the community.
  • My Requests / Votes / Comments: All submitted ideas, votes, and comments can be found here.

Are you ready? Well, then we are waiting for you!

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Ensure Seamless SAP Operations: Update your S-User Passwords Across All Destinations
entry.content:
In SAP products like SAP Cloud Connector and other SAP Cloud solutions, S-users and their passwords are stored in various destinations by our customers.

It's crucial to remember that any password change must be updated in all these destinations to maintain seamless operations and avoid locking of S-users.

While you can request SAP to unlock S-users without issue, by creating a case on SAP for Me component XX-SER-FORME, this does not resolve problems caused by outdated passwords in destinations on long-term. Failure to update passwords can lead to repeated lockouts. SAP does not provide a tool to identify where S-users are blocked due to incorrect login data and that makes it tricky.

Key Reminder:

  • Track where your S-users are stored.
  • Always update passwords in all relevant destinations when changes are made.

By doing this, you'll save time and make your workday a lot smoother!

 

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Come contattare il team di support di SAP Education – Learning Hub
entry.content:
** This blog was translated by my Italian speaking colleague Petra Sunjic

State cercando supporto dal Learning Hub o dal Training Team e non sapete da dove cominciare? Questo blog è per voi…

In questo articolo, condividerò i passaggi che dovete seguire per interagire con l'area di supporto; gli agenti sono disponibili 24 ore su 24, dal lunedì al venerdì, e la maggior parte delle richieste riceve risposta entro 24 ore.

Cliccate qui se desiderate leggere questo blog in inglese, tedesco o spagnolo.

Nella piattaforma di supporto troverete una sezione dedicata alle FAQ, che offre risposte rapide alle domande più frequenti.

  • Temi generali di supporto
  • Sistemi di pratica
  • Formazione per partner rivenditori
  • Consigli su apprendimento e certificazione
  • Prenotazione corsi e gestione dei pagamenti
  • Pannello di analisi

Come creare un caso

Se le informazioni che sta cercando non sono presenti nella piattaforma, può creare un caso per ricevere supporto per la sua richiesta specifica.

Basta accedere con il Suo Universal ID e creare un ticket.

Dal sito SAP Learning Hub:

  1. Cliccate sull'icona del punto interrogativo (?) situata in alto a destra della pagina
  2. Nella pagina del Centro di supporto, cliccate sul pulsante “Contattaci” e selezionate “Richiedi assistenza tecnica”.
  3. Scegliete l’opzione “Avete bisogno di più aiuto?”
  4. Effettuate il login con il vostro Universal ID e cliccate su “Crea caso”.

Dal sito SAP Learning:

  1. Scorrete fino in fondo alla pagina e cliccate su “Ottieni supporto”, che si trova nella sezione Supporto e Contatti.
    .
  2. Sarete reindirizzati alla pagina del Centro di supporto utenti, dove troverete le FAQ e guide dettagliate per l’auto-aiuto.
  3. Se non trovate l’aiuto necessario nelle FAQ, scorrete verso il basso e cliccate sull’icona “Avete bisogno di aiuto?” per accedere alla pagina successiva e aprire un ticket.
  4. Completate la procedura cliccando sul pulsante “Crea caso”.

Inoltre, potete utilizzare il link diretto per accedere al Centro di supporto utenti.

Per visualizzare gli aggiornamenti del vostro ticket, vi consigliamo di cliccare sul pulsante di aggiornamento prima di cercare il vostro caso.

In che modo il Learning Team può aiutarvi?

Il Learning Team può aiutarvi se desiderate ottenere informazioni su corsi, contenuti di certificazione e percorsi di apprendimento. Inoltre, può consigliarvi su quale apprendimento sia necessario per raggiungere i vostri obiettivi.

Altri servizi includono:

  • Domande di primo livello sulle certificazioni, inclusi consigli su come configurare correttamente il vostro UID e accedere alle credenziali.
  • Problemi relativi a informazioni, consegna e assistenza post-corso per le sessioni condotte da istruttori.
  • I problemi relativi alle prenotazioni e alla fatturazione sono generalmente gestiti da un altro team interno, ma il team di apprendimento può intervenire per approvare eventuali nuove prenotazioni gratuite e situazioni simili, in quanto spesso sono legati a reclami da parte dei clienti, ecc.
  • Lettere per il visto.
  • Fornire informazioni relative ai fornitori su richiesta.

Richedere supporto in una lingua diversa.

Se desiderate che il team di apprendimento vi assista nella lingua preferita, potete selezionare la lingua dopo aver inserito la descrizione, selezionando il paese in cui vi trovate e scegliendo tra: Tedesco, Inglese, Spagnolo, Francese e Giapponese.

Vi invitiamo anche a leggere il nostro blog per scoprire tutto sulle certificazioni SAP e sull’Universal ID - SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! 

Inoltre, questa KBA potrebbe esservi utile: 2404504 - Come ottenere aiuto con il SAP Learning Hub o il SAP Training Center.

Grazie per aver letto!

entry.title: Expert-Guided Implementation: Selective Data Transfer (from SAP Solution Manager) to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello!

 

As you may already know, the mainstream maintenance for SAP Solution Manager 7.2 is available until the end of 2027. To avoid any disruption in your IT processes, SAP recommends all customers to transition to SAP Cloud ALM before that.

To help you during this transition (from a solution documentation perspective), SAP Enterprise Support is offering a new pilot Expert Guided Implementation (EGI) service through the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council (ESAC): “Selective Data Transfer to SAP Cloud ALM”.

This EGI will give you access to a methodology and process to find out how this tool works and what object types are supported (depicted in the picture below).

 

This is a three-day remote session where an SAP Expert explains the Selective Data Transfer methodology in detail. On the same day, participants are expected to execute the demonstrated steps within their own SAP environment, and, if necessary, receive direct guidance and support from the SAP Expert.

 

During this EGI, we will demonstrate the results of a sample Readiness Check analysis for SAP Cloud ALM, focusing on the Selective Data Transfer aspects. In addition, we delve into the SDT process and tool, which will guide and assist you throughout the transition. Relevant content and entities such as the process hierarchy and core business process models are manually uploaded to target SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

 As a pre-requisite, ensure you have access to your own SAP Cloud ALM tenant and SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SP15 (or above) system. If you don’t have one tenant yet, request it today at SAP Cloud ALM. To get further information about the fair usage rights, visit Usage Rights

 For further queries including how to join these pilots, reach out to the SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council directly at mailto:esac@sap.com.

 

Happy Learning!

entry.title: SAP Organizational Change Management Starterkit – Zusammenfassung der Kaffee-Ecke Januar 2025
entry.content:
Zum Jahresauftakt 2025 stellte Anja Scheurer, Senior Change Management Beraterin bei SAP, das Organizational Change Management (OCM) – Starter Kit vor.  Dieses Framework dient als Hilfestellung bei der Implementierung von Organizational Change Management (OCM) in SAP-Projekten.

In der Kaffee-Ecke haben wir zum Check-In gefragt wer der Teilnehmenden ein OCM Framework in der Organisation hat und nutzt. Laut Poll haben nur 30% ein solches Framework.

Das Starterkit ist in Form eines digitalen Buches im SAP Enable Now Info Center zugänglich und beinhaltet:

  • Detaillierte Erläuterungen zu OCM-Methoden und -Prozessen
  • Einsatzbereite Vorlagen und Templates für verschiedene OCM-Aktivitäten
  • Schrittweise Anleitungen zur praktischen Umsetzung von Change Management

Mit einem S-User und Universal ID kann es jeder kostenfrei ansehen und herunterladen. SAP Enable Now Kunden können es zudem importieren und anpassen.


Vorstellung des SAP OCM Startkits

Das Starterkit orientiert sich an den verschiedenen Dimensionen des OCM Framework der SAP welches die wesentlichen Tätigkeiten im Change Management beschreibt.

  • Change Strategie
  • Change Leadership
  • Change Kommunikation
  • Change Realisierung
  • Change Effektivität


Verwendung des OCM Starterkits

  • Einstieg zu Beginn des Projekts für die Staffing und Scoping
  • Aufklärung über die Change Methodik im Projektteam
  • Befähigungsmaterial für Change Agents


Hauptbestandteile des Starterkits

Anbei eine kurze Zusammenfassung der verschiedenen Themenbereiche und was jeweils wichtige  Aufgaben sind.

Change-Strategie

  • Durchführung eines umfassenden Change Assessments zur Bewertung der Ausgangssituation
  • Erstellung eines detaillierten Konzepts und Plans für das Change Management im Projekt

Change-Führung

  • Durchführung einer gründlichen Stakeholder-Analyse zur Identifikation und Bewertung relevanter Interessengruppen
  • Aufbau und Management eines effektiven Change Agent Netzwerks zur Unterstützung der Veränderungsprozesse

Change-Kommunikation

  • Entwicklung eines strukturierten Kommunikationsplans für alle Projektphasen
  • Nutzung verschiedener Kommunikationskanäle zur zielgruppengerechten Informationsvermittlung

Befähigung & Training

  • Planung und Durchführung von zielgerichteten Trainings zur Vorbereitung der Mitarbeitenden auf die Veränderungen

Effektivitätsmessung

  • Regelmäßige Durchführung von Umfragen zur Bewertung der Wirksamkeit der Change-Maßnahmen
  • Implementierung von Readiness Checks vor dem Go-Live zur Überprüfung der Bereitschaft
  • Durchführung von User Adoption Messungen nach dem Go-Live zur Evaluierung der tatsächlichen Nutzung

Datengestütztes Change Management - Deep Transformation Insights (DTI)

Zentral und bildlich in der Mitte des Modells ist der Umgang mit Daten. Dabei hilft der Service “DTI” ein übergreifenden Verständnisses des aktuellen Zustands aus Perspektive der Mitarbeitenden zu erlangen und datengestützt Change Maßnahmen abzuleiten.

Nutzung eines Dashboards zur periodischen Messung und Visualisierung von Mitarbeiter-Sentimenten. Sentimente sind Meinungen und Empfindungen. Sie werden im Framework in die Bereiche Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, Commitment gruppiert.

  • S. auch einen Podcast zum Thema DTI


Diskussion und Fragen

Findet man hier irgendwo die Zuordnung zu den SAP Activate Phasen?

  • Noch nicht im Starterkit, jedoch im Activate Roadmap Explorer in der OCM Sektion – siehe der Blog Professionelles Change Management mit dem SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer 

Ist eine Integration von Learning Services wie Lern-Bedarfsanalyse als Teil des Starterkits gewünscht?

  • Diese Frage von Anja wurde von mehreren Teilnehmenden ausdrücklich bejaht – es ist gewünscht.

Welche Definition zwischen Projektmanagement Leistungen und Change Management Leistungen empfiehlt sich im Sinne einer Rollenschärfung?

  • Üblicherweise gibt es Projektmanagement Verantwortliche, und OCM Verantwortliche in SAP Einführungsprojekten. Eine klare Abgrenzung der Verantwortlichkeiten zwischen Projektmanagement und Change Management ist hilfreich.
  • Durchführung einer detaillierten Rollendefinition zu Projektbeginn, idealerweise unter Verwendung einer RACI-Matrix
  • Vollständige Integration der Change Management-Aktivitäten in den übergeordneten Projektplan
  • Hier eine Tn Stimme: Ich habe hauptsächlich mit Consultants zu tun, die sich individuell mit den Unsicherheiten, Bedenken und Widerständen und "Arbeitsverweigerungen" der Projektbeteiligten auf Kundenseite herumschlagen müssen. Abteilung z.B.). Falls OCM im Projekt nicht im Orgchart vorkommt, können solch zentralen Verantwortungen helfen.

Einschätzung des Anteils von SAP-Projekten, die ohne strukturiertes OCM durchgeführt werden

  • Sehr viel mehr Projekte aus meiner Sicht beachten OCM, aber bei weitem noch nicht alle. Manche der Change Verantwortungen werden aber durch zentrale, Projekt unabhängige Rollen, übernommen (Kommunikationsverantwortliche für die Abteilung z.B.). Falls OCM im Projekt nicht im Orgchart vorkommt, können solch zentralen Verantwortungen helfen.

Wie können wir noch mehr Stakeholder überzeugen, in Change Management zu investieren

  • Das ist regelmäßig ein wichtiges Thema. Der datenbasierte objektive Ansatz kann schonmals helfen.
  • Nutzung von Erfahrungen und Lessons Learned aus früheren Projekten zur Untermauerung der Argumentation für OCM
  • Einsatz kreativer Ansätze wie Storytelling, um die Bedeutung und den Mehrwert von Change Management zu verdeutlichen


Inspirierende Aussagen

"Change Management ist kein reines IT-Projekt, sondern ein ganzheitliches Business-Projekt."

"Der Mensch ist der entscheidende Faktor, der ein System letztendlich zum Leben erweckt."

"Selbst die beste technische Umsetzung kann sich als kostspielige Fehlinvestition erweisen, wenn sie von den Mitarbeitern nicht angenommen wird."

"Als Change Manager müssen wir auch versierte Storyteller sein, um die Notwendigkeit des Wandels überzeugend zu vermitteln."

"Die Aussage 'Wir brauchen kein Change Management' gehört zu den potenziell teuersten Fehleinschätzungen in Projekten."

Anja geht das auch gerne humorvoll an und stellt z.B. als Icebreaker in Meetings die teuersten Sätze der Welt vor.


Resümee

Das SAP Change Management Starterkit bietet eine umfassende und praxisorientierte Unterstützung für organizational Change Management in SAP-Projekten. Es deckt wesentlichen Aspekte des Change Managements ab und stellt konkrete, anwendbare Werkzeuge zur Verfügung.

Die Diskussion verdeutlichte, dass Change Management nach wie vor eine signifikante Herausforderung in vielen Projekten darstellt, jedoch zunehmend als kritischer Erfolgsfaktor anerkannt wird. Nutzer-Feedback ist herzlich Willkommen um weitere Angebote an Kunden zu gestalten.

Zum Zugriff benötigt man einen allgemeinen Nutzer, den sich jeder anlegen kann. Wer noch keinen SAP User hat: anbei gibt es Informationen zur Universal ID.


Weiterführende Links

Das Organizational Change Management (OCM) – Starter Kit

SAP Change Basecamp Workshop:  Veränderungsmanagement erlernen und anwenden am praxisnahen Beispiel. Der Workshop ist nun buchbar für den 9 - 10 April, 26 - 27 Juni, 22 - 23 Oktober sowie am 26 - 27 November.

Neue Learning Journey: Acting as Professional Change Manager in SAP Cloud Projects

Die nächste Kaffee-Ecke findet zum Thema adaptive Transformation mit Eike Wagner in der Change Hut im Zillertal statt – Anmeldung findet ihr wie immer hier auf der Website der Kaffee-Ecke.

entry.title: Warum Change Management bei einer SAP Cloud Migration wichtig ist - ein Blick in die Praxis
entry.content:
In der aktuellen Folge des EducationNewsCast Podcast diskutierte ich mit Philipp Blaesing von Siemens Healthineers und Anja Scheurer von SAP die entscheidende Rolle des Change Managements bei SAP Cloud Migrationen, insbesondere im Rahmen des RISE-Programms. Hier könnt ihr reinhören auf 🎧 SAP Podcasts - Apple - Spotify. Anbei eine Zusammenfassung als Blog. 

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers: Eine globale IT-Initiative

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers ist eine der größten IT-Initiativen des Unternehmens. Es umfasst die Migration von über 20 SAP-Systemen und angrenzenden Applikationen in die SAP Private Cloud.

Philipp Blaesing betont: "Innovation hat bei uns einen hohen Stellenwert bei Siemens Healthineers. Und da in Zukunft alle Innovationen in der RISE Cloud entwickelt werden, hat natürlich auch die Migration bei uns eine sehr hohe Visibility und Wichtigkeit."

Technische Migration vs. Geschäftsprozess-Transformation: Ein zweistufiger Ansatz

Ein interessanter Aspekt des Projekts ist die Trennung von technischer Migration und Geschäftsprozess-Transformation. Blaesing erklärt: "Wir trennen die technische Migration von den Business-Transformationsprojekten. [...] Erst ein stabiles Fundament gießen, wenn man mal so eine Metapher von einem Hausbau sieht und dann kann man die Räume draufbauen, wie man sie will."

Obwohl es sich primär um ein technisches Projekt handelt, unterstreicht Anja Scheurer die Bedeutung des Change Managements:

"Auch wenn RISE-Projekte auf den ersten Blick eben nur als ein technisches Projekt scheinen, betreffen technologische Veränderungen, technologische Transformationen immer die Menschen, die sie dann schlussendlich auch natürlich anwenden müssen."

Die Rolle des Change Managements in technischen Migrationsprojekten

Die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Projekt- und Change Management ist eng verzahnt. Anja Scheurer erläutert: "Grundsätzlich arbeiten wir eng auf Programmebene, sowohl aber auch eben auf der Projektebene mit den jeweiligen Verantwortlichen zusammen." Das Change Management-Team fungiert als Cross-Workstream im Programm und gibt die Methodik und den Change- und User-Kommunikationsplan auf Programmebene vor.

Die Verbindung von Change- und Projekt Management 

Change Management und Projektmanagement waren bei der SAP Cloud Migration von Siemens Healthineers eng verzahnt:

  • Integration: Change Management als Cross-Workstream im Gesamtprogramm.
  • Klare Rollen: Deutliche Aufgabenteilung zwischen PMO und Change Team.
  • Operative Einbindung: Change-Experten in Projektterminen und Cutover-Meetings.
  • Kommunikation: Während das PMO die interne Programmkommunikation vorantrieb, fokussierte sich das Change Management Team auf die Nutzer- und Nutzenkommunikation.
  • Ressourcenbereitstellung: Das Change Management Team stellte den Projektleitern Werkzeuge und Materialien zur Verfügung, um Change-Aktivitäten umzusetzen.

Diese enge Zusammenarbeit ermöglichte eine durchgängige Berücksichtigung von Change-Aspekten und schnelle Reaktionen auf Herausforderungen im Projektverlauf. 

Bewährte Methoden und Tools im Change Management

Bei den eingesetzten Methoden und Tools hat sich das Team anfangs am ADKAR-Modell orientiert, dieses aber an die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des Projekts angepasst. Bewährt haben sich klassische Stakeholder-Analysen, Engagement-Pläne und Change-Readiness-Checks. Blaesing betont auch die Wichtigkeit eines guten People-Networks und vielfältiger Kommunikationskanäle.

Learnings und Tipps für erfolgreiche SAP Migrationsprojekte

Als wichtigste Lernerfahrung nennt Philipp Blaesing die frühzeitige Einbindung des Change Managements:

".. auf jeden Fall das Change Management so früh wie möglich zu involvieren, also schon in den Planungsphasen." Anja Scheurer ergänzt: "Unterschätzt den Bedarf einfach überhaupt nicht für das Change Management und der Kommunikation in Migrationsprojekten, bindet eben die Kollegen und Kolleginnen früh ein."

Auch gaben beide konkrete Tipps für die Praxis:

  • Eine klare RACI-Matrix für Change- und Kommunikations-verantwortlichkeiten erstellen
  • Vielfältige Kommunikationskanäle nutzen (Newsletter, Webseiten, Q&A-Sessions ... )
  • Regelmäßige Feedback-Runden mit Stakeholdern durchführen
  • Change Management als offiziellen Cross-Workstream im Programm etablieren

Inspirierende Aussagen aus dem Podcast

"Wenn man das Gefühl hat, man kommuniziert zu viel, dann war es richtig."
"Change Management ist sehr viel mehr in den Mittelpunkt gerückt als vor fünf bis zehn Jahren und gewinnt immer mehr an Wichtigkeit."

Resümee

Die Erfahrungen von Siemens Healthineers zeigen, dass eine frühzeitige Einbindung, klare Strukturen und vielfältige Kommunikation entscheidend sind. 

Was ich wichtig finde ist auch folgendes: Change Management erweist sich auch bei primär technischen Projekten als wichtiger Erfolgsfaktor. Die Handlungsfelder sind vielleicht etwas anders. So gibt es beispielsweise wenig End-User Training. Aber Änderungen die Prozesse, Jobs und Aufgaben betreffen gibt es auch bei technischen Projekten. Und auch Fragen zum Mehrwert eines solchen technischen Projekts und die Besprechung und Kommunikation dieser Vorteile. Und hier ist eben Change Management sehr wichtig mit all seinen Facetten, von Stakeholder Analysen bis Kommunikation

⁉️ Habt ihr weitere Beispiele, was bei sehr technischen Projekten besonders ist rund um Change Management? Freue mich auf Kommentare hier auch. 

Shownotes

Buch: Die 5 Sprachen der Mitarbeitermotivation

Buch: Der Change Code von Dieter Lederer

Doku der Community-Session mit Anja zum SAP Change Starterkit

SAP News: Siemens Healthineers entscheidet sich für RISE with SAP um Leistung zu steigern

Aus-und Weiterbildung bei Siemens Healthineers

entry.title: How to add an additional user or e-mail address to the SAP Universal ID (UID)
entry.content:
You have an SAP Universal ID Account and need guidance on how to add an additional user or email address? Don’t worry, we’ve got you covered with this straightforward guide!

 

If you already have an existing SAP Universal ID, you can easily add additional email addresses in the SAP Universal ID Account Manager and link associated S-user or P-user ID(s) using the below simple steps.

 

Here’s how: 

  • First, login to your SAP Universal ID Account Manager: https://account.sap.com/.
  • Next, click on "Add Account" in the upper right corner of your screen: ￼

 

 

 

 

  • Enter the e-mail address associated with the S/P-user ID(s) you want to link and click "Submit".

 

  • Then, an email with a verification code is sent to your email address. ￼

 

  • Confirm your submission by entering the verification code that has been sent to your email address. ￼

 

  • If you have an S-user/P-user ID(s) associated with your email address that you would like to link. Click the "Link" button next to the S-user/P-user ID(s) you would like to link: 
  • When all S-user/P-user ID(s) or e-mail addresses are linked, click X button to Exit.

For more details, please refer to KBA: 2882908 - How to add an additional user or e-mail address to the SAP Universal ID (UID) - SAP for Me

 

Note: As per KBA 3360451, a maximum number of 10 e-mail addresses can be linked, and a maximum number of 100 users can be linked with your SAP Universal ID account.

During SAP Universal ID registration (see KBA 2882963), after you verify your e-mail address, you will be forwarded to the Linking Manager. The Linking Manager will retrieve the IDs that match the e-mail address that you have verified. If no IDs are found, click the "link accounts" button. That way you can link your additional email addresses with their S/P-users IDs from the very start.

 

Reminder:

An email address (with or without associated S-/P-User) can be linked to only one SAP Universal ID.  If the email address is already a part of another SAP Universal ID account. The email can't be added to another one and SAP Universal IDs cannot be merged.  The relevant email address from the existing SAP Universal ID needs to be removed or delete the entire duplicate SAP Universal ID first before you can add the email address to another SAP Universal ID account.

For more details and process on how to request in unlinking S-user/P-user ID(s) or email address, and or delete the entire duplicate SAP Universal ID, please refer to the KBA 2914815 or, SAP BLOG: SAP Universal ID: Unlinking if email is already li... - SAP Community

 

Adding a Recovery Email Address for Certification Requirements

In relation to certifications. It is mandatory that a secondary email address must be added in your SAP Universal ID Account Manager for recovery purposes. Adding a secondary email address for recovery purposes is essential to meet certification requirements and ensure your credentials are always accessible.

  • Your recovery email address must be under a different domain than your primary Universal ID email address.
  • The secondary email address does not have to be linked to a P/S-user account in order to qualify as a recovery/secondary email address.
  • Please note an email address that has no S/P-user ID associated with it & is linked to a Universal ID, will show in the Not Linked section of your SAP Universal ID Account Manager. (This is expected behavior & is successfully linked).

If you face any issues related to SAP Certification or courses, you can reach out to the SAP Education/Learning Team through this SAP Blog: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub t... - SAP Community.

 

Helpful KBAs & Links:

 Here are some additional resources to help you with your SAP Universal ID:

  • Helpful Tips from the Customer Interaction Center – SAP Universal ID 
  • SAP Universal ID - FAQ 
  • Creation and Benefits of Universal ID 
  • KBA: 2962360 - How to change the primary e-mail address - SAP Universal ID (UID) - SAP for Me
  • How to reset your Universal ID password - SAP Community
  • SAP News Center 

 

Got More Questions?

Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!  

 

Alternatively, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

 

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming webcasts on https://events.sap.com/supportwebcasts/en/home.
These sessions are open to all the users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, Universal ID, etc. Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.

 

Thanks for reading! We hope you found this guide helpful.

entry.title: Preventive Support for SAP’s New Era of Customer Care
entry.content:
A few years ago, mobile phones did not have functions that nowadays we consider part of our routines such as video chat and music streaming. Today’s smartphones, moreover, have evolved to include integration of functions like camera, mobile payment and various apps. Similarly, customer care approaches have developed along with cellphones. Historically, the inability of businesses to afford real-time analysis meant that customer care was mostly reactive, dealing with issues as they arose.

In an era of real-time data, this backward-looking strategy presents a challenge companies need to overcome to understand customer behavior and boost business performance. Organizations now aim to retrieve information from past, present and future to gain a comprehensive view of their processes to forecast events and identify anomalies. Consequently, the outcomes expected will be the result of anticipated preparation and planning.

At SAP, the vast repository of historical issues, combined with extensive product knowledge and expertise, forms a rich foundation for preventive support. Some of the best practices to implement successful preventative customer care involve:

  • Using robust automation systems and AI models to process issue signals quickly.
  • Providing self-service opportunities through knowledge bases and chatbots.
  • Offering proactive support through notifications and updates.

AI and Machine Learning, alongside various data points and automation, enable us to identify patterns and issues, and to implement automated mitigation actions to prevent customers' business impact at early stages.

Furthermore, the potential of insights from critical event data as a strategic asset has expanded its use beyond traditional areas like marketing and sales. Now, departments such as customer service, finance, and operations management are also adopting this strategy. To assess the effectiveness of our customer care efforts, SAP employs several key metrics, including:

  • Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS).
  • Customer Effort Score (CES) to rate how effortlessly customers were able to receive support.
  • Reduction in support calls or inquiries.
  • Production uptime and availability.

In this way, we monitor our progress while refining algorithms based on real-world feedback and performance metrics. For our customers, preventive support means an increase in reliability of their system that will lead to customer satisfaction. Additionally, they adapt to changing scenarios, therefore decision-making process will be based on real-time information.

Having successfully exercised this approach to support SAP Commerce Cloud customers during 2024 Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales events, we are at the moment expanding this model across other SAP products.

Just as smartphones have become essential through the integration of diverse functionalities, our preventive support approach seeks to be crucial in proactive customer care. As we continue to refine our AI models and expand our prognostic capabilities, we're committed to staying at the forefront of innovative solutions that keep businesses running smoothly.

At SAP Sapphire 2025, which will take place in Orlando and Madrid, we’ll hold sessions with experts to show how we support customers to better predict issues.

Check out this SAP News Center article to learn how proactive customer care can drive success during peak e-commerce sales periods.

A special thanks to SAP experts Bharath Ramamurthy, Vice President of Strategic Initiatives, Customer Support, and Martina Kiely, Business Lead of Technology & Transformation Office, Customer Support Experience, for their contributions to this article.

entry.title: Continuous Quality Checks (CQCs) for SAP IBP Implementation
entry.content:
Embarking on a project can be both exciting and daunting. One of the key aspects of a successful implementation is ensuring the quality and stability of your solution. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to support this goal. 

What are Continuous Quality Checks?

Continuous quality checks (CQC) are remote services that analyze your system and/or specific situations to help reduce system risks and outline optimization potential. You will receive a report, including an executive summary with findings and guidance for improvement.

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP IBP

To support your SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) implementation project, the following continuous quality check services are available:

  1. CQC for Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP - Prepare* — The latest CQC service offering that helps review the project team’s awareness of key SAP IBP topics, ensuring that you are in the right direction at the early stage of the project.
  2. CQC Going Live Support — To help you through a smooth start of production.
  3. CQC Deployment Readiness — For a detailed analysis of your SAP IBP before your go live.
  4. CQC Business Process Performance Optimization (CQC BPPO) — For a performance analysis of your business critical process steps for optimal performance improvement.

Requesting Remote Services: When and How to Get Started

The diagram below highlights the recommended timings for requesting the CQC services, aligned with the SAP Activate Methodology phases, from Discover to Run. 

For information about CQC services for supply chain management, including how to request them, visit the Continuous Quality Checks* section of our Supply Chain Management Solutions value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Get Moving with SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

Included with every SAP cloud subscription, value maps offer structured, product-level Learning Rooms where you can get personalized guidance for your SAP IBP journey.

Access Value Maps in 3 Easy Steps:

  1. Sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support (please allow 2 hours for processing).
  2. Access value maps via the Learning Hub.
  3. Navigate to the SAP Enterprise Support value maps for Supply Chain Management Solutions. Once in the value maps, you can collaborate in forums, ask questions, or “Request a Call” to speak with one of our experts for further assistance.

Value maps are intuitive to navigate, but we recommend scheduling an onboarding call to tailor the experience to your specific needs and highlight the most relevant content.

Got a question about value maps or our content?

  • Use the “ask questions” feature in the value maps.
  • Or, contact us at: sapesvaluemaps@sap.com

Brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support value map team for Supply Chain Management Solutions.

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Customer Support at SAP’s Customer Support Summit – Register Now!
entry.content:
Join us on Thursday, March 27, 2025, at SAP’s Newtown Square office for an exclusive Customer Support Summit—an event designed to inspire, inform, and connect SAP customers and partners with the latest innovations in customer support and cloud lifecycle management.

Why Attend?

Customer support is evolving rapidly, and SAP is leading the way with cutting-edge AI-powered solutions, enhanced self-service tools, and strategic support frameworks. At this summit, you'll gain insights into how SAP’s latest advancements can help you optimize operations, enhance system performance, and drive business success.

What’s in Store for You?

  • Special Guests: Geoff Scott, CEO and Chief Community Champion at ASUG and Dave Lehanski, Executive Vice President, Business Development & Innovation at NHL.

  • Keynote by Mohammed Ajouz – Get inspired by SAP’s Senior Vice President, Global Head of Technical Support, as he shares the vision for SAP Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management.

  • Thought-Provoking Panel Discussion – Hear from SAP leaders, customers, and partners on the future of customer support, AI-driven automation, and the next wave of agentic support solutions.

  • Tech Talks & Demo Pods – Experience hands-on demonstrations of SAP’s self-service tools, AI-powered support solutions, and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

  • Networking Reception – Connect with fellow professionals, industry experts, and SAP executives in an engaging and interactive setting.

Who Should Attend?

This free-of-charge event is tailored for SAP customers and partners who want to stay ahead in the ever-evolving support landscape. Whether you’re exploring RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, or looking for ways to optimize your support strategy, this summit offers invaluable insights and direct access to SAP experts.

Don’t miss this opportunity to engage, learn, and connect with the best in the industry.

Secure your spot today – Register now!

entry.title: How Data driven Change Management with Deep Transformation Insights ensures successful SAP Business
entry.content:
This blog highlights the critical role of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in the successful implementation of SAP S/4HANA. It delves into how SAP's Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service leverages data-driven insights to help organizations manage change effectively, ensuring employee acceptance and engagement throughout the transformation process.

The Challenge of SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Change Management Perspective

Implementing SAP S/4HANA is a transformative process that goes beyond a mere technological upgrade. It reshapes business operations, IT systems, and employee behavior while embracing a Fit-to-Standard mindset This increased focus on standards helps to reduce the time and costs associated with implementation and support, and also drives business efficiencies as well as scalability. It is SAP’s recommended approach to enable faster adoption of SAP S/4HANA.

Despite the recognized importance of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in ensuring successful business transformation, many organizations struggle to integrate it effectively into their strategy. We recently did a Change Assessment with more than 800 respondents on the perception of different important OCM related dimensions. See here some highlights:

Key Challenges in OCM Implementations

Strategic Alignment and Ownership

  • Confusion about who should take ownership of OCM within the organization
  • Lack of clarity in defining desired business outcomes
  • Misconceptions about OCM's scope and responsibilities

Leadership Involvement

  • 64% of companies acknowledge senior leaders' crucial role in driving change
  • Only 56% report active engagement of leaders with their teams
  • Lack of visible support undermines morale and alignment

Communication and Change Story

  • Only 48% of organizations actively communicate SAP program updates
  • 54% have a clearly defined Change Story

Resistance to change evolves when discussions focus around Fit-Gap keeping the current business processes in the future system and NOT focusing on fit-to-standard.

Past Experiences and Skepticism

  • Poor outcomes in previous OCM efforts lead to skepticism
  • Some organizations exclude OCM from new projects due to past experiences

Resource Constraints and ROI Measurement

  • Lack of internal structures and expertise for effective OCM implementation
  • 71% believe their Change Management Strategy explains the SAP program's importance
  • Only 47% feel end users are equipped to apply SAP solutions in daily work
  • OCM is often viewed as an optional expense rather than a critical success factor
  • Difficulty in justifying additional spending on OCM
  • Only 44% of organizations actively assess the effectiveness of their change initiatives
  • Lack of clear metrics to demonstrate ROI

Overcoming these challenges requires a holistic approach to OCM, emphasizing clear ownership, leadership involvement, effective communication, and measurable outcomes. By addressing these key areas, organizations can maximize the benefits of their SAP S/4HANA implementation and ensure a smoother transition for all stakeholders.

To address such challenges the Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service from SAP comes into play. With this data driven approach, organizations can safeguard the success of their SAP S/4HANA implementations. It's not just about deploying new technology; it's about preparing the people who will use it, ensuring sustainable adoption, and unlocking the full potential of the transformation.

DTI is built around five key dimensions or sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—that provide a structured path for guiding individuals through change, fostering both readiness and long-term engagement. Let’s look at these briefly.

Research-based Change Sentiments to Accelerate Change and Adoption in SAP Implementations

Based on research conducted by organizational scientists like A. Armenakis, this five important factors have been identified as they determine the degree of employee buy-in by organizational change recipients.

Awareness

  • Foundation of successful transformation
  • Ensures recognition of the need for change
  • Focuses on communicating the "why" behind the change
  • Aligns change initiative with broader business goals

Acceptance

  • Builds on awareness
  • Addresses emotional and intellectual readiness
  • Focuses on trust-building and reducing resistance
  • Creates psychological safety for employees

Enablement

  • Bridge between readiness and action
  • Equips individuals with necessary tools, training, and resources
  • Creates a supportive environment for adapting to new roles
  • Minimizes disruption and maximizes productivity during transformation

Empowerment

  • Fosters ownership and confidence among employees
  • Grants authority for decision-making within respective roles
  • Cultivates a culture of distributed decision-making
  • Inspires innovation and accountability at all levels

Adoption and Commitment

  • Emphasizes alignment between personal and organizational goals
  • Prioritizes intrinsic and extrinsic motivation
  • Focuses on recognition and reinforcement
  • Ensures continued engagement, real productive usage of the new processes or tools

 

From an SAP perspective, we assembled these factors and change sentiments into a framework aligned with the SAP Activate methodology and focused on SAP systems implementations.

We call this framework Deep Transformation Insights (DTI), utilizing change sentiment questionnaires throughout the implementation cycle for customers. DTI contains assessment tools that can be administered at any stage of the implementation process.

The insights obtained can serve as:

(a) An indicator of the degree of adoption/acceptance among users

(b) An assessment of issues in project enablement that can adversely impact the success of the implementation

(c) A basis for planning and executing learning to enhance user adoption

(d) A regular data driven pulse-check to monitor success of actions to improve the five dimensions.

Customer Case highlighting Activities from Data-Driven Insights to Accelerate User Adoption in SAP S/4HANA Projects

So how could this look like in SAP projects? To make it tangible we would like to share a real world customer case.   

The customer prepared for a planned implementation of SAP S/4HANA supporting an enterprise-wide digital transformation strategy to enhance scalability and efficiency. Further business alignment goals included greater simplicity, efficiency, insight, collaboration, transparency, and compliance, among others.

The company achieved success by prioritizing employee buy-in and minimizing disruption. Using SAP's Deep Transformation Insights service, they enhanced their change management with digital tools and data-driven insights. This people-centric approach effectively managed the transformation's complexity, leading to a successful implementation After implementing the DTI service, the company established and orchestrated an employee engagement feedback architecture using a structured change-sentiment framework across project and organizational units.
Additionally, they gained better insights into stakeholder perceptions and experiences throughout the project lifecycle. They now use these insights as the foundation to accelerate and support solution adoption.

"Utilizing DTI was seamless, and the insights we gained from the team were very thorough, thoughtful, and actionable. We were able to easily see that the work we were doing was having the intended effect because of the results from DTI"
Director Organizational Change Management, Kyndryl

Summary

Organizational Change Management (OCM) is crucial for ensuring successful adoption and realizing the full potential of any digital transformation. However, many organizations face challenges in integrating OCM into their implementation strategy.

Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) offers a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing a data-driven approach to change management that aligns with the SAP Activate methodology. By focusing on five proven key sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—DTI helps organizations guide their employees through the change process, fostering engagement and long-term success. Find out more about SAP Organizational Change Management on sap.com, or contact us per e-mail.

We look forward to discuss your questions in the comments.

entry.title: How to activate your S-User ID
entry.content:
Log in to SAP for Me and start exploring everything the platform has to offer today! 

                                     

A company that has a support contract with SAP and a Super Administrator set up within the company can create your S-User ID. You’ll receive an automatically generated welcome e-mail. This email will allow you to activate your S-User which can then be linked to your Universal ID.   

For whatever reason your S-User activation may have expired or perhaps you accidentally deleted the activation e-mail.  

Here are the steps to follow for an alternative activation method:

  1. Go to Forgot My Password.  

  1. Enter your S-user ID and click continue.   
  2. Check your inbox for an e-mail sent from SAP ID Service (ias@notifications.sap.com) to your S-user e-mail address.  
  3. Open the e-mail and click on the password reset link.  

 To note:   

  • The password reset link is valid for 2 hours from the time that it was generated from the system and can only be used once. If the password link expires, repeat the steps 1–4.   
  • You can only change your password once in a 24-hour period. 

  Now your S-User has been activated it will be available to add to your Universal ID! 

 Check out this blog to add your newly activated to your Universal ID: SAP Universal ID: Tips and Tricks - SAP Community    

For more information on S-User ID’s take a look at this blog: Learn All About S-User IDs! - SAP Community  

 Troubleshooting Tips:   

If you haven’t received the password reset e-mail after a few minutes:  

  • Check your SPAM/junk folder.  
  • Clear your browsing history, cookies, and cache and repeat the steps. 
  • Check that your company email settings are not blocking emails from SAP.  

 If you still haven't received the e-mail: Contact the SAP ID Service Team (SSO Team) at the following email address: sso@sap.com. You'll see more details on the SSO Team here: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service Team

                                                                                                                                                       

Thanks for reading!   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please ask a questions in the Q & A using All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

Read more ##CustomerInteractionCenter blog-posts here!

entry.title: Did you know: SAP Support Log Assistant 2.0 Self Service Portal? & Trace errors for your IPS logs
entry.content:
SAP Support Log Assistant 2.0 is a powerful tool designed to streamline the analysis of support-related files such as logs, traces, and configuration files. This tool leverages the expertise of SAP Product Support to provide automated solutions and recommendations for known issues.

Try it now - Support log assistant standalone, self-service tool

SAP Video - Support Log Assistant - Self Service Tool Demo

SAP Knowledge Base Article: 2838708

How It Helps: The Support Log Assistant 2.0 helps users by:

  1. Automated Analysis: It scans text-based files for known issues and provides recommendations based on SAP's extensive knowledge base
  2. Efficient Troubleshooting: By identifying and highlighting important details in the files, it accelerates issue resolution without the need to open a support case
  3. Time-Saving: Reduces the manual effort required for log analysis and solution searches, allowing users to focus on other critical tasks

Benefits and Advantages:

  1. 24/7 Availability: The tool is available round-the-clock, providing expert-level log file analysis at any time
  2. Accelerated Resolution: Speeds up the process of resolving issues by automatically identifying problems and suggesting solutions
  3. PDF Reports: Generates detailed PDF reports of log analysis findings and recommendations, which can be shared with peers to extend best practices
  4. Standalone Access: Users can access the tool as a standalone application via the SAP for Me customer portal
  5. Enhanced Collaboration: Facilitates sharing of analysis results with peers, promoting collaborative problem-solving

Use Cases:

  1. Enterprise Data Management: Analyzing log files related to data management systems to identify and resolve issues quickly
  2. SAP Identity Provisioning Logs : Analyzing provisioning job files downloaded to identify and resolve issues quickly with Source & Target Systems defined or Transformation logic issues
  3. HANA Platform: Troubleshooting HANA database-related problems by scanning configuration and trace files
  4. Business Intelligence: Ensuring the health of BI systems by automatically detecting and addressing log file errors
  5. Big Data: Managing and resolving issues in big data environments through automated log analysis
  6. NW JAVA and NW Core: Supporting NetWeaver Java and Core systems by providing solutions to log file issues.

FAQs for Support Log Assistant

SAP Identity Provisioning Service - Provisioning Logs -  Download Logs to analyze

After uploading the log files, Scanning will start and provide you the initial analysis with list of Alerts which is causing the issue of failed and also provides SAP Note KBA details as well

Summary will provide the graphic representation to Download Analysis Report 

Conclusion: 

The SAP Support Log Assistant is an invaluable tool for customers, Partners & SAP users, offering automated, expert-level analysis of support-related files. Its ability to accelerate issue resolution, save time, and facilitate collaboration makes it a must-have for maintaining a healthy SAP environment.
